Football.title: Kepa Arrizabalaga hints he is winning the race to be Arsenal’s No1 in Sunday’s Carabao Cup final
Football.content:
KEPA ARRIZABALAGA has hinted he is winning the race over Arsenal No1 David Raya to start in Sunday’s Carabao Cup final.

The Spanish keeper, 31, has been Raya’s back-up for Mikel Arteta’s Quad-chasers this season but has started every League Cup tie.





Arteta has a decision to make ahead of the Wembley showpiece against rivals Manchester City.

Kepa is no stranger to the Carabao Cup final after he famously refused to be substituted before Chelsea’s penalty shootout defeat to Pep Guardiola’s side in 2019.

But the Gunners stopper has hinted that he will get the nod over Raya as he discussed how he plans to prepare for the Wembley pressure.

Kepa said: “The atmosphere at Wembley is so special, everything around the game is special – the build-up and everything.

“You know you are out there playing for a title, and right now in this moment of football, every title is so difficult to get.

“You know you are one game away from being able to lift a trophy. Obviously, it’s going to be a tough game, a very difficult game, but we’ll go for it.

“I have played in the League Cup final before, but I have played many finals in my career, I have been lucky in that way.

“So I will just approach the game the best way that I can. We will prepare everything in the best way, because at the end the aim is to win.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“Just focus on the game, focus on us and prepare for the opponent. That’s it. I don’t think we need to approach anything differently.”

Arsenal can pick up their first trophy of the season with victory over Manchester City under the Wembley arch.

Tuesday night’s 2-0 win over Bayer Leverkusen means Arteta’s men are now in the Champions League quarter-finals, as well as being nine points clear at the top of the Premier League and into the FA Cup quarters too.



Kepa, who joined Arsenal from Chelsea in a £5m deal this summer, reckons the final against City could be the perfect springboard to ensure more trophies follow before the season ends.

He added: “We have big players who have played in big games, in finals, in the Euros, in the World Cup, in the Champions League. We know what it is to play in big games every three days.

“Manchester City have great players in every position, they are one of the strongest teams without a doubt. They have a lot of different options and depth of quality.

“For us though, hopefully this could be a way for us to open our trophy cabinet, and to keep it open.”

The Carabao Cup is often used as a momentum builder for teams, with the winner going on to lift more silverware in seven of the last 12 seasons.

Football.title: Andy Carroll named Dagenham & Redbridge manager as KSI brutally sacks boss – and he faces Prem legend in first game
Football.content:
DAGENHAM have sacked manager Lee Bradbury less than two weeks after YouTuber KSI took over the club – and under fire former Premier League ace Andy Carroll has been put in charge.

His first task will be against veteran boss Neil Warnock and Torquay United on Saturday.





Bradbury, 50, was axed on Wednesday after a 3-2 win over Farnborough on Tuesday night.

He had only been in the post since the summer but lost his first match after Olajide “JJ” Olatunji, aka KSI, rocked up, drawing the second and then finally winning his third and last.

Former England star Carroll, 37 has been appointed as his replacement.

It comes as Carroll is set to stand trial next January after pleading not guilty to a charge of breaching a non-molestation order.

The footballer is accused of “repeatedly” making phone calls to ex-wife Billi Mucklow, who he shares three children with, in March last year.

The former couple married in 2022 but announced they were divorcing two years later.

A non-molestation order is an injunction issued by a civil court, usually aimed at preventing a person from going near another individual, address or both.

People who breach one, depending on the circumstances, can face criminal action resulting in a jail term of up to five years.



Carroll himself is new to the club having signed on a free from French side Bordeaux last summer.

Journeyman striker Carroll played 12 times for the Daggers in the National League South this season, scoring six goals.

He has been out since mid-December, though, and will now find himself in the dugout against someone with over 1,000 career matches in the hot seat.

More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online

Thesun.co.uk is your go-to destination for the best celebrity news, real-life stories, jaw-dropping pictures and must-see video.

Football.title: Outspoken Zlatan Ibrahimovic announced as World Cup 2026 pundit as fans say ‘this is going to be intense’
Football.content:
ZLATAN IBRAHIMOVIC has been announced as a pundit for the 2026 World Cup.

The Swedish superstar, 44, retired from football back in 2023.





That brought the curtain down on an incredible 24-year career which saw him win 32 trophies, play for some of the biggest clubs in the world and feature at the 2002 and 2006 World Cups.

Ibrahimovic racked up 122 caps for Sweden and a record 62 goals.

But with his country’s involvement at this summer’s tournament still to be confirmed – they will need to beat Ukraine then Poland or Albania in this month’s play-offs without Alexander Isak – Zlatan will be there.

The legendary striker revealed he will take up TV punditry duties for US broadcaster Fox Sports with a brilliant advert.

The minute-long commercial is set at a cafe in a deserted square in Milan in the wake of the Winter Olympics – with Ibra sipping on a coffee and providing the voiceover.

Ex-AC and Inter man Zlatan says: “Where has everybody gone? The Olympics is over but that is no reason to be sad and depressed.

“It felt good, no? Cheering for your red, white and blue… all the gold medals, it was nice.



“But the snow is melting now and you don’t know what to do. You want to watch the best in the world.

CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“I bring the American people some good news.

“Not only is the Fifa World Cup coming to Fox this summer… so is Zlatan.

“See you soon and you’re welcome, America.”

The clip has various snapshots from the recent Milano Cortina Games plus quick highlights of Lamine Yamal, Cristiano Ronaldo, Erling Haland, Kylian Mbappe and US hero Christian Pulisic celebrating plus Lionel Messi kissing the trophy at Qatar 2022.



Zlatan finishes off the video holding the adidas 2026 World Cup football up to the camera before a message reads: “Zlatan joins the Fox World Cup team – June 11th.”

And after a career littered with bold, confident and sometimes arrogant quotes, fans cannot wait to see what Ibrahimovic will be like in the studio as a pundit.

Click on a World Cup stadium to find out more

One said: “It is going to be intense.”

Another wrote: “Zlatan didn’t join the broadcast, the World Cup joined Zlatan. Man commentates like he’s still scoring goals.”

A third added: “When Zlatan Ibrahimovic says ‘you’re welcome’ you just know the 2026 Fifa World Cup coverage is about to be legendary.”



A fourth typed: “Zlatan doesn’t join Fox, Fox joins Zlatan.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

A fifth commented: “Now this is something I would love to see.”

And a final user replied: “World Cup just became must-watch television.”

Football.title: Declan Rice silences Micah Richards with savage seven-word jibe on live TV and leaves fellow pundits in stitches
Football.content:
DECLAN RICE ruined Micah Richards as the pair served up some on-air verbals before the Carabao Cup final.

Rice’s Arsenal take on Richards’ former club Manchester City in a high-stakes Wembley showcase this Sunday.





And Richards – on punditry duty for CBS – began the mind games after Arsenal’s Champions League win over Bayer Leverkusen on Tuesday.

He said to Rice: “I love you as a player… but we’ll see you on Sunday, eh? We’ll see you on Sunday.

“Just make sure you’re ready on Sunday because City are coming through!”

Richards then chanted: “CITY! CITY! CITY!”

But Rice did not miss a beat as he clapped back: “I thought he was an Arsenal fan?”

Studio pundits Thierry Henry and Jamie Carragher fell about laughing at Richards’ awkward moment.

The City academy product and two-time Premier League winner is an official club ambassador for the Blues – but has previously been exposed as a childhood Gunners supporter.

With Rice grinning like a Cheshire cat, Carragher shouted: “He’s just lost his ambassadorial gig!”



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Richards clarified where his loyalties lie in an interview earlier this year.

He said: “I’d like to right a few wrongs. I said, and if you look at programs [from] when I was at Man City years ago, I always said I was an Arsenal fan. But I’m not.

“I absolutely adore – and this is hand on heart, we’re being serious now – I absolutely love Man City so much and what they’ve done for me.



“But I can’t help who I support.”

Richards scored 10 goals in 245 City appearances and played for Fiorentina and Aston Villa before retiring in 2019 and becoming a big-name pundit.

Football.title: Seven managers Chelsea could turn to including Fabregas and Alonso as pressure piles on Rosenior
Football.content:
LIAM ROSENIOR’S position at Chelsea has been called into question after the club’s Champions League exit.

The Blues fell to a 3-0 defeat, 8-2 on aggregate, to Paris Saint-Germain in their last 16 clash against the French champions.





That has coincided with Chelsea falling out of the top five of the Premier League after a run of one win in five, putting their hopes of playing Champions League football next season into jeopardy.

Fans made their feelings about the recent dip known as boos rang around Stamford Bridge on Tuesday night, and his decision to sub off Enzo Fernandez, Joao Pedro and Cole Palmer at 2-0 did not help matters.

The club’s hierarchy are now reportedly questioning the long-term future of the 41-year-old, whom they only hired in January.

SunSport have looked at seven possible replacements for the ex-Hull and Strasbourg boss if he is given the boot.



Xabi Alonso

Out of the managers currently available on the market, Blues chiefs would be hard-pressed to find one better than Xabi Alonso.

The Spaniard guided Bayer Leverkusen to an incredible unbeaten Bundesliga title and DFB Pokal double, while they did also lose the Europa League final to an Ademola Lookman hat-trick for Atalanta.

He eventually took over at Real Madrid, but left the club in January 2026 after a 3-2 defeat to Barcelona in the Supercopa de Espana.



The 44-year-old looks destined to join old club Liverpool as pressure mounts on Arne Slot, but Chelsea could make a huge offer to try and tempt the former midfielder to change destination.

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Ruben Amorim

Prior to his time at Manchester United, Ruben Amorim was seen as one of the brightest up-and-coming managers in Europe.

He built Sporting Lisbon back up into a title-winning force after decades in the wilderness, moulding a team full of youth and energy into champions.

Amorim, 41, joined United with hopes of doing the same, but a stubborn belief in his tactics ultimately led to his downfall before an explosive press conference finally tipped him over the brink and saw him sacked.



Wiser from his failure and still with youth and a good track record overseas in the bank, Chelsea could gamble on Amorim being able to grow with the team and turn them from chumps to champions.

John Terry

By contrast, Chelsea bosses could make a move for a club legend such as John Terry to immediately get fans onside.

Terry, 45, won five Prem titles with the Blues, and that experience could be important to developing the current squad.

The former club captain admitted he was left “frustrated” after not being considered for a role assisting Calum McFarlane during his period as interim following Enzo Maresca‘s departure.



He is looking for a job in management after a number of unsuccessful interviews with EFL clubs, so he would be available at the drop of a hat should Chelsea chiefs come calling.

The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube Calling all Chelsea fans - we have a brand new YouTube channel! The Sun’s Chelsea YouTube channel will be your go-to destination for the latest news, transfer updates, match previews, post-match analysis, exclusive interviews, fan reactions, and breaking stories from Stamford Bridge. Subscribe to The Chelsea Blueprint on YouTube now

Frank Lampard

Sticking with the club legend theme, Frank Lampard has previously been in the Chelsea hotseat and did reasonably well.

Results did fall off in his second season, but the squad he helped to build based on the backbone of the Cobham academy did go on to win the Champions League that season under successor Thomas Tuchel.

Lampard spent time in management at Everton before he returned to Chelsea as caretaker after Graham Potter was sacked, and he is now seven points clear at the top of the Championship with Coventry.



Like Terry, Lampard has limitless fan support from his playing days at the club, and maybe a few more years of experience in the industry could make all the difference from his previous showing at the club.

Filipe Luis

Another Chelsea title winner who could be considered by BlueCo is in Filipe Luis.

He was bizarrely sacked by Brazilian giants Flamengo following an 8-0 win and with an incredible haul of seven trophies won in 100 games, including the Copa Libertadores and Brazilian league title.

Chelsea chiefs spoke to Luis about the possibility of becoming the club’s next boss after Maresca left, and not long after the Brazilian was out of a job himself.



Globo claim he is in line to become the manager of Cruzeiro, so if Chelsea wanted to see the 40-year-old return to the Bridge they would need to move fast.

Gary O’Neil

Former Bournemouth and Wolves manager Gary O’Neil was named as Rosenior’s replacement at Strasbourg, Chelsea’s sister club under the BlueCo multi-club ownership model.

If Rosenior is given the boot by Chelsea it stands to reason that they would once again be inclined to look internally first, which would leave them eyeing O’Neil.

The former Premier League star, 42, did well as Bournemouth’s interim manager and managed a run of five wins from seven at the end of his first season to save the Cherries from the drop.



He had a less successful spell at Wolves, but given his past Prem experience it arguably puts him in a better position as a candidate than Rosenior.

Cesc Fabregas

Another Chelsea title winner with Luis, Terry and Lampard, Cesc Fabregas is currently the manager of Italian minnows Como.

The Spaniard was part of a takeover of the team in 2022 and has managed the team as they have bulldozed their way up the Italian leagues.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

That has put him on the radar of some of Europe’s top sides, including his former club Chelsea.



The 38-year-old is another manager who could come in and build a project.

Football.title: Watch Ian Wright’s incredible reaction to Eberechi Eze goal for Arsenal as he sits with huge music star at Emirates
Football.content:
ARSENAL legend Ian Wright celebrated wildly after Eberechi Eze’s thunderbolt yesterday.

The retired striker watched on alongside a rising star of British music.





Wright, 62, was visibly elated after Eze opened the scoring for Arsenal against Leverkusen on Tuesday night.

He raised his arms in the air and turned around to those seated close to celebrate.

The Gunners’ legend was joined by singer Myles Smith in a hospitality box.

This has led fans to believe he is also an Arsenal fan despite being seen wearing a Celtic shirt last year.

The Gunners went on to win 2-0 on the night – with Declan Rice scoring the second.

His was also a great strike as the Gunners sealed safe passage into the Champions League quarter-finals with a 3-1 aggregate win.

Their favourable side of the draw will see them face Sporting.

The Portuguese outfit sensationally came back to beat Bodo/Glimt last night.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

They trailed 3-0 to the Norwegians before winning the second leg 5-0 after extra-time.

This brought Bodo’s fairytale run to an end.

Arsenal are currently the bookies’ favourites to win their first Champions League at odds of 9-4.

Football.title: English football club left ‘significantly out of pocket’ as part of stadium DISMANTLED in payment row
Football.content:
A NON-LEAGUE football club has lost a substantial amount of cash after a stand at their stadium was torn down.

Sittingbourne are in the Isthmian South East Division play-off places and gunning for promotion having narrowly missed out last season.





But the Kent-based side have suffered a serious blow after a stand next to the Staxson Stadium dugouts was dismantled due to a third party supplier not being paid for materials.

And Sittingbourne have revealed the payment row has left them with a financial predicament.

A club statement read: “Sittingbourne Football Club can confirm that we no longer have the Bourne to Win scaffold stand, located to the left of the pitch.

“The club can confirm that we paid for an additional stand in good faith, unaware that a third party supplier had not been paid for materials relating to its construction.

“Sittingbourne Football Club has no contractual relationship with this third party and was not made aware of their involvement at any stage.

“As a result, the club has incurred the full cost of the stand but has now had it dismantled by the third party due to him not receiving payment, leaving the club significantly out of pocket.

“The club are currently reviewing the situation and will consider its next steps.”

Sittingbourne are assessing their options after forking out for the scaffold-based structure.



SUN VEGAS WELCOME OFFER: GET £50 BONUS WHEN YOU JOIN

The Brickies had former Liverpool talent Jordon Ibe on their books earlier in the campaign.

But the winger’s stay at the Staxson Stadium only lasted three games in the space of EIGHT DAYS.

Ibe, 30, ended up heading through the exit door to join Bulgarian top-flight side Lokomotiv Sofia.

Sittingbourne only missed out on promotion to the seventh tier of English football thanks to a penalty shoot-out defeat in the play-off final last season.

They also shattered a club record by reaching the FA Trophy quarter-finals before losing to National League side Aldershot.



And the Brickies, under boss Reece Prestedge, are now 18 points adrift of table-topping Three Bridges.

Football.title: Liverpool vs Galatasaray referee was forced to apologise after far-right event speech
Football.content:
Liverpool vs Galatasaray referee was forced to apologise after far-right event speech

The Polish referee will officiate Liverpool vs Galatasaray at Anfield on Wednesday night

View 2 Images Szymon Marciniak was forced to apologise for his attendance at a far-right event (Image: JONATHAN NACKSTRAND/AFP via Getty Images)

A top-tier referee who previously had to apologise for his association with a far-right event will officiate Liverpool vs Galatasaray on Wednesday night. Szymon Marciniak will be behind the whistle at Anfield as the Reds strive to overturn a 1-0 first-leg defeat against the Turkish giants.

‌



Marciniak will no doubt face some challenging confrontations in the middle as Arne Slot's team battles for one of their remaining chances of silverware this season. However, the Polish referee has dealt with larger controversies in the past, having been compelled to apologise for attending a business gathering linked to a far-right politician.

‌



In 2023, following his appointment by UEFA for the Champions League final between Manchester City and Inter Milan, Marciniak issued a statement.

‌



The Warsaw-based anti-racism organisation Never Again had alerted the governing body to the 45-year-old's presence and participation in an event associated with Slawomir Mentzen, a leader of the far-right Confederation party.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Diogo Jota's widow in touching Liverpool tribute ahead of difficult new milestone READ MORE :

Champions League co-efficient rankings latest as Man Utd and Liverpool sweat on extra spot READ MORE :

Marciniak, who memorably officiated the 2022 World Cup final, claimed he was "gravely misled and completely unaware" of Mentzen's connections to the conference. In a statement released by UEFA, the referee said: "I want to express my deepest apologies for my involvement and any distress or harm it may have caused.

‌



"I had no knowledge that [the conference] was associated with a Polish extreme-right movement. Had I been aware of this fact, I would have categorically declined the invitation.

View 2 Images Marciniak has officiated some of the biggest games in recent history (Image: Julian Finney/Getty Images)

"I am committed to learning from this experience and ensuring that such lapses in judgment do not occur in the future."

‌



UEFA noted Never Again's acceptance of Marciniak's apology at the time and confirmed that he would referee the European final. They stated that "removing him would undermine the promotion of anti-discrimination."

The governing body added: "Based on the information provided, UEFA confirms that Mr. Marciniak will fulfil his role as the referee for the 2023 UEFA Champions League final."

Watch Liverpool vs Galatasaray on TNT Sports This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more £30.99 TNT Sports Get TNT Sports here Product Description Liverpool have reached the UEFA Champions League round of 16 where they face Galatasaray on March 18 . Fans can watch the game on the TNT Sports Prime Video channel.

Article continues below

Polish sports minister Kamil Bortniczuk backed Marciniak throughout this difficult period, stating that the referee delivered a 45-minute speech with a "strictly business, motivational and inspirational character" that had nothing to do with Mentzen.

Strengthening his defence on social media, Marciniak told his Instagram followers that he had "never supported nor legitimised any political party, organisation or individual politician."

Since this contentious incident, Marciniak has officiated at the FIFA Club World Cup and served as fourth official for Spain's 2-1 victory over England in the UEFA Euro 2024 final. Three years later, the Polish ref will take the main job for Liverpool vs Galatasaray with the Reds' European progress on the line.

Football.title: Dagenham SACK boss just weeks after KSI buys club with ex-Premier League star now in charge
Football.content:
Dagenham SACK boss just weeks after KSI buys club with ex-Premier League star now in charge

KSI was named as a minority shareholder at Dagenham & Redbridge on March 3 but just 15 days later, their manager has been sacked and Andy Carroll has been named as his replacement

View 2 Images Dagenham & Redbridge are on the search for a new manager weeks after KSI's investment. (Image: Getty Images)

National League South side Dagenham & Redbridge have sacked manager Lee Bradbury, with YouTuber-turned-media personality KSI making his first major decision as minority shareholder. Former Liverpool and Newcastle United striker Andy Carroll has been named manager on an interim basis.

‌



KSI, whose real name is Olajide Olayinka Williams Olatunji, was announced as a minority shareholder of the former Football League club at the beginning of March. Just two weeks on, Bradbury has been relieved of his duties at the Daggers after seven months in charge. The decision comes with the club six points outside the playoffs and less than 24 hours after a 3-2 win away at Farnborough.

‌



The club, who play in the seventh tier of English football - two rungs below League Two, said in a statement: "Dagenham & Redbridge Football Club can confirm that we have parted company with First-Team Manager, Lee Bradbury.

‌



FA ban disgraced former GP from refereeing football matches after social media posts READ MORE :

Jeremy Jacquet injury timeline emerges as Liverpool 'panic' over injury recovery time READ MORE :

"We are very grateful to Lee for his focus, professionalism, and commitment during his time at the Club and for his contributions, both on and off the pitch, to stabilise the club last summer.

"Dagenham & Redbridge wish him every success in his future endeavours. Andy Carroll will take the team on an interim basis, and further updates will be communicated in due course."

Carroll boasts 248 Premier League appearances, scoring 54 goals across a career spanning Newcastle, Liverpool and West Ham United.

‌



JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

The 37-year-old was Dagenham & Redbridge's talisman after joining in July, but it has since been announced that he has become one of their latest shareholders.

In February, the National League South club were taken over by Happy Fan Group LLC, an investment group led by John Grabowski. Then KSI was confirmed as a shareholder and strategic partner at the beginning of this month with the 32-year-old setting out ambitions to take the non-league club to the Premier League.

‌



Taking inspiration from Wrexham's hit Disney+, KSI will film a docuseries about the club in a 'Race To The Top' series that will be released this summer on the YouTuber's channel.

The 32-year-old said: "I've done boxing, music, YouTube content, I've been around the world, I'm a man that does whatever he wants, you can never stop doing what you want. I'm going to buy Dagenham & Redbridge Football Club.

‌



"The idea excites me so much. Years ago, I saw the happiness, the joy, I want to bring that back and surpass it. I want the place to be pumping, I want the place to be rocking, be an event when you come here.

"Playing a video game and owning a football club is very different but with Race to Division One it was a journey it was hard but in the end, I did it.

"With this club I want to do the unthinkable, I want to take Dagenham & Redbridge to the Premier League. It's going to take time but realistic stages, I think getting out of this league and entering the National League.

‌



"We need to improve the team, I have improvements to take it to another level."

View 2 Images KSI celebrates during his first match involved with Dagenham & Redbridge as they beat Dorking Wanderers 1-0. (Image: Getty Images)

He added: "I know to some of you it might be scary, me being one of the owners, but I'm reachable. You can tweet me and I will see it. You can let your frustrations out or celebrate with me.

Article continues below

"I'm a fighter. I've broken my nose, broken my hand, broken my thumb, I've been in tough fights but with the Daggers, I'm going to keep fighting so that we can succeed. I'm just ready to get started."

Football.title: Man City face cannon over 115 charges after Chelsea walk into police station with hands up
Football.content:
Man City face cannon over 115 charges after Chelsea walk into police station with hands up

Chelsea have earned leniency from the Premier League - but Manchester City have not and should get the book thrown at them if found guilty

View 3 Images Chelsea deserve commending - but Man City don't (Image: Getty Images)

In the murky, oil-slicked waters of Premier League financial dealings, honesty is usually the first casualty. We're talking about a league where creative accounting is treated with the same reverence as a 30-yard screamer, and where the rulebook is often viewed more as a list of polite suggestions than anything binding - or, God forbid, something that actually carries consequences when broken.

‌



So, when the news dropped that Chelsea had been slapped with a fairly modest £10.75million fine and a one-year suspended transfer ban for the 'secret payments' of the Roman Abramovich era, the pitchforks were sharpened with predictable speed.

‌



Fans all over the country were understandably baying for blood. After all, Everton, Nottingham Forest and Leicester City have been treated like Victorian pickpockets, hauled before the magistrates and stripped of points while the Premier League looks on with the cold, unblinking eyes of a debt collector.

‌



But here's the hard truth that many won't want to swallow: Chelsea deserve their leniency. Not because they're innocent - they're as guilty as Wesley Fofana at a speed awareness course - but because they had the stones to walk into the police station, put their hands up, and tell the truth.

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Chelsea to face second punishment from FA for breaking rules after record fine issued READ MORE :

Chelsea star Pedro Neto learns UEFA decision on ban after shoving PSG ball boy READ MORE :

When Todd Boehly, Behdad Eghbali and Clearlake Capital took over in 2022, they swung open the doors of every dusty, creaky cupboard in search of skeletons. And rather than bury them under the Shed End, they dragged them into the light, inviting investigators to take a proper look.

‌



By self-reporting, by co-operating, Chelsea should be applauded, not thrown to the wolves. Even if you disagree with that notion, disincentivising honesty is about as counterproductive as Chelsea's goalkeeping department.

And this is where we need to talk about the elephant in the room. Or rather, the 115 elephants.

While Chelsea have opted for a strategy of radical transparency - effectively scrubbing the Siberian grease off the walls of Stamford Bridge - Manchester City have taken a rather more... defensive and outright aggressive approach to the 115 Financial Fair Play charges hanging over their heads like a dark raincloud.

‌



View 3 Images Chelsea have been fined £10.75m and have received a one-year suspended transfer ban for 'secret payments' made during Roman Abramovich's tenure (Image: Adam Davy/PA Wire)

They're currently OJ Simpsoning their way through the greatest legal battle in English football history, hoping to buy their freedom via a super team of high-priced, ethically-elastic lawyers. While Chelsea have willingly handed over their ledgers, City have coated theirs in Tipp-Ex and coffee stains, constructing a legal Great Wall of China designed to frustrate, delay and obfuscate.

City insist they've done nothing wrong, of course. But their behaviour suggests otherwise. The bookkeeping dead ends and bureaucratic smoke-bombs they've conjured aren't the actions of the innocent or the misunderstood - it's the white Ford Bronco of financial defences: a slow-motion, highly-publicised flight from the truth.

‌



There's a nauseating shamelessness about the way City are operating. The bully-boy tactics, the cynical gamesmanship, the industrial-scale time-wasting - it's the legal iteration of Arsenal's 2025/26 on-field dark arts! It's a strategy of attrition. They aren't just fighting charges, they're fighting the very idea that anyone has the right to charge them in the first place.

The contrast couldn't be more stark. Chelsea are the reformed rogues, hat in hand, ready to face up to their sins in a bid to turn over a new leaf. City, meanwhile, are the billionaire moguls who, when slapped with a parking ticket, are happy to spend a king's ransom trying to bankrupt the local council.

We should want clubs to do what Chelsea have done. We should want new owners to arrive, smell the rot, and start the clean-up. If the Premier League had docked Chelsea 10 points for self-reporting, nobody would ever come forward again. The league would remain a dark, windowless room where the most successful teams are simply the ones with the best shredders.

‌



View 3 Images Manchester City have been flagrantly opposed to the investigation into their finances (Image: Getty Images)

Leniency for the honest is the only way to keep the game remotely clean. But that logic must work both ways. If Chelsea are the poster boys for co-operation, then City must be the example of what happens when you refuse to play ball.

If any of those 115 charges end up being proven, the Premier League cannot afford to pull their punches. There can be no suspended transfer bans or drop-in-the-bucket fines for a club that has spent years thumbing its nose at the authorities with a such a flagrant disregard.

Article continues below

Chelsea acted like adults. They took their medicine, and yes, it was a spoonful of sugar compared to what others have swallowed. But they earned that sweetness by being upfront.

As for City? If they are found guilty, the book shouldn't just be thrown at them. It should be fired out of a cannon.

Football.title: FA ban disgraced former GP from refereeing football matches after social media posts
Football.content:
FA ban disgraced former GP from refereeing football matches after social media posts

Anti-vax medic David Cartland - who was struck off for harassment, threats and fraud - has vowed to appeal against the decision

View 2 Images David Cartland has been barred from refereeing football games by the FA and DBS (Image: David Cartland/X )

A doctor who has been struck off the medical register is now prohibited from working with children or officiating football matches. David Cartland has been banned by both the Cornwall Football Association and the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS).

‌



Mr Cartland has taken to X to inform his over 300,000 followers about the decision by the two organisations to bar him. The DBS ban stops him from working with children or vulnerable individuals, a restriction he says prevents him from driving his neighbour's children to football.

‌



He asserts "no crime has been committed nor conviction made, no allegation of harm to children or vulnerable adult was ever made" and he is appealing both decisions. The FA states that anyone who is barred from regulated activities with children is not permitted to referee football in any capacity involving under-18s as this is considered regulated activity.

‌



It says: "The Football Association (FA) is legally required to prevent people who are barred from working with children from becoming involved in football in under-18s. Whilst the restriction is specifically for children's football, if your ban stems from a conviction that leads to a football banning order, you may also be restricted from attending or participating in matches at stadiums (including adult football)."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

A few weeks ago, Mr Cartland shared the decision letter from the DBS - which also resulted in him being prohibited from refereeing - on X with his followers. The ban stems from his actions on social media platforms, which led last year to him being struck off from the General Medical Council (GMC) registers, meaning he can no longer practice medicine in the UK.

‌



Liam Rosenior feeling the pressure as Chelsea fans turn and Enzo Fernandez aims dig READ MORE :

The letter states: "Whilst most adults in every day situations are not considered to be vulnerable, any individual in receipt of health care is considered a vulnerable adult. It is reasonable to conclude from the information made available, that you, in your capacity as a doctor advised individuals and issued medical exemptions, and by definition, those adults are considered to be vulnerable adults.

"Consequently, given the behaviour you have demonstrated, if you were to continue to work or volunteer in regulated activity with vulnerable adults you may consider it acceptable to act in a way which you see fit for yourself, aligns with your own beliefs or is intentionally harmful irrespective of the impact on those who may be reliant on you.

"The behaviour you have demonstrated has shown that you may falsify documentation and you may be of the belief that you are entitled to ignore and flout policies and protocols, particularly when you consider your own personally held beliefs may warrant this and therefore fail to consider your professional obligations."

‌



The letter goes on: "You have also demonstrated that you may actively go against the advice given to you and there is no evidence of insight into your conduct. When your behaviour is considered overall, it is considered to pose a considerable safeguarding risk if you were to continue to work or volunteer in regulated activity with vulnerable adults. We are therefore satisfied that it is appropriate to include you in the Adults' Barred List."

For the same reasons, the DBS also placed Mr Cartland on its Children's Barred List. It was the DBS's decision which led the FA to bar Mr Cartland from refereeing football matches. However, in a post on X, Mr Cartland questioned his DBS and FA ban, asking: "Why am I banned from refereeing veteran football? Super veteran football? Open age football? Playing football?? These are not regulated activities. Let alone in the majority not involving under 18."

In a post on March 2, he added: "The reason I referee is more than just a hobby. It's a passion. A passion for the beautiful game. When my legs gave up re playing, it was a natural next step. In my playing career of over 32 years I never received a single yellow card sanction. I've refereed over 100 games since."

‌



On Sunday, March 15, Mr Cartland also posted: "A family man who has lost his career, the slander and libel about him, unjustifiably put on a list among criminals and dangerous adults, and eventually his hobby and passion of refereeing grassroots football. All for your non-agreement on scientific matters that I have been absolutely correct and evidence-based upon.

"When I am correctly compensated for this whole debacle I will promise to identify every single one of you anonymous cowards and make you face the law and civil repercussion of your fixated obsessive cowardly behaviour. I have a list. So do the police."

View 2 Images Mr Cartland is an anti-vaxxer who was struck off from the medical register

‌



There is no evidence of any action being taken by Devon and Cornwall Police on behalf of Mr Cartland, who was struck off the GMC while his name was removed following 17 days of hearings by the Medical Practitioners Tribunal Service (MPTS) in June last year. The tribunal determined that he published or reshared numerous messages across social media platforms X, Gettr, Telegram, Instagram and WhatsApp and harassed a consultant obstetrician, a locum consultant in paediatric A&E at a children's hospital, a viral immunologist and a former operational practice manager at a health centre and encouraged others to do so.

Mr Cartland was found to have set up an X account using another doctor's identity and that some of his posts were driven by his hostility towards the LGBTQ+ community. The tribunal also determined that Mr Cartland threatened one of the doctors he harassed online with retaliatory action after she reported his conduct to the GMC.

The former doctor, from the Penzance area, was also found to have contacted an individual on WhatsApp offering to supply a Covid exemption certificate and that he would record a 'medical' exemption for anyone, regardless of whether they had a medical exemption or not.

‌



In doing so, he intended to create a medical record containing information he knew may be false. When he received an email from a medical practice manager informing him that a job application he had submitted would not progress because, as a proactive Covid vaccination centre, they believed that his anti-vaccine stance contradicted their principles, Mr Cartland posted screenshots of this email on his Gettr and X accounts.

They included the practice manager's personal contact details, which the GMC said was intended to encourage other social media users to harass her. He has over 300,000 followers on X. At the time, Mr Cartland claimed his actions on social media were about "freedom of conscience and free speech" while the actions of the tribunal had attempted to restrict such freedoms.

He accused the GMC of singling out doctors who had reservations about the safety and effectiveness of the Covid-19 vaccine roll out programme and also accused his accusers of "harassing him in a raucous online environment." During the hearings - which Mr Cartland did not attend - his representatives argued the three other doctors he harassed online should have been "charged" and said he felt isolated and "under siege."

‌



His argument was that what he termed anti-consensus doctors required protection and criticised the GMC's position, alleging that it had been using the tribunal process to "silence him and his views." He also made baseless accusations that the three doctors conspired with each other, and others, online to harass him. He was struck off after the tribunal judges determined that 17 allegations against him by the GMC were substantiated.

On his website, Mr Cartland asserts he can continue to offer help and support to individuals because he is "still a doctor, still knowledgeable, still ethical, still compassionate, still advocating for patients' safety and still medically qualified." Regarding his football and DBS ban, Mr Cartland, who frequently shares his anti-vaccination theories on X and other platforms, has received backing from medical colleagues who hold similar views.

In an 'open letter' posted online, which he has been promoting on X, addressed to Dr Jeff James, CEO, and Dr Gillian Fairfield, chair of the DBS and to Sue Ravenlaw, Football Association head of safeguarding, the signatories from the Children's Covid Vaccines Advisory Council (CCVAC), Doctors for Patients UK (DfPUK), Health Advisory and Recovery Team (HART) UK and Medical Freedom Alliance, wrote: "Dr Cartland has not only lost his livelihood because of the GMC decision but now, with your totally disproportionate and unjust decision to place him on the barred lists, has also lost his place as a football referee.

‌



"Despite refereeing, unlike coaching, not being a regulated activity, as it never involves unsupervised contact with children or vulnerable adults. The FA have also told him that he cannot play adult football and suggested he could be barred from being a spectator at junior matches.

"Football spectators are never screened for their DBS status, so this is clearly a cruel singling out of Dr Cartland for special punitive measures and cannot be allowed to happen. The DBS decision is a complete travesty of justice against a highly experienced and ethical doctor with an exemplary record of patient care and is a gross overreaction. His language on X was legal and would have attracted no comment had he not been questioning the safety of the Covid vaccines."

Mr Cartland has alleged he is the victim of a smear campaign designed to damage his reputation due to his anti-vaccination stance and other opinions. In a statement to CornwallLive, he confirmed he is challenging the DBS ruling and FA ban.

Article continues below

He said: "No crime has been committed nor conviction made, no allegation of harm to children or vulnerable adult was ever made during the MPTS hearing, and the tribunal's late reference to him being 'considered to pose a risk to children' is not only untrue, unevidenced but is firmly disputed before the Upper Tribunal."

The DBS and the FA declined to comment on the matter.

Football.title: Kepa vows to open Arsenal's trophy cabinet...and keep it open in quest for quadruple
Football.content:
Kepa vows to open Arsenal's trophy cabinet...and keep it open in quest for quadruple

Arsenal's No 2 Kepa Arrizabalaga is in line to start for the Gunners at Wembley on Sunday, as they look to land the first leg of an unprecedented quadruple by beating Manchester City

View 3 Images Mikel Arteta is expected to name Kepa as his starter at Wembley this weekend (Image: Adrian Dennis / AFP via Getty Images)

Arsenal keeper Kepa Arrizabalaga says the Gunners want to open their trophy cabinet on Sunday - and keep it open for the rest of the season.

‌



The Gunners are still in the hunt for a remarkable quadruple and have a chance to strike a blow against their biggest title rivals Manchester City at Wembley.

‌



Gunners No2 Kepa, who is set to play in the Carabao Cup final, believes success in the Carabao Cup final can give them a taste for winning - and put them in the mood for more success.

‌



Arsenal are nine points clear of City at the top of the Premier League, they are into the Carabao Cup final and have a quarter final FA Cup tie at Southampton ahead of them.

They also reached the Champions League quarter finals while City crashed out and a win on Sunday could also strike a real confidence blow and lay down a marker for the rest of the campaign.

OPINION John Cross Eberechi Eze produces more big-match magic in Arsenal and Mikel Arteta's pivotal week

‌



Arsenal's path to Champions League final suddenly becomes clearer READ MORE :

Kepa said: “We have big players who have played in big games, in finals, in the Euros, in the World Cup, in the Champions League. We know what it is to play in big games every three days.

“Manchester City have great players in every position, they are one of the strongest teams without a doubt. They have a lot of different options and depth of quality. For us though, hopefully this could be a way for us to open our trophy cabinet, and to keep it open.”

Kepa has played in two League Cup finals before but finished on the losing side in 2019 and 2022 well before his move to Arsenal. He is now understudy to Arsenal No1 David Raya but has played through the rounds in the domestic cups and is fully expected to start Sunday’s final.

‌



Kepa, the penalty shot-out hero in the quarter final win over Crystal Palace, will bring his own experience into the game and is preparing to relish the opportunity.

View 3 Images Kepa was the hero as the Gunners beat Crystal Palace earlier in the competition (Image: Clive Mason/Getty Images)

He added: “The atmosphere at Wembley is so special, everything around the game is special - the build-up and everything. You know you are out there playing for a title, and right now in this moment of football, every title is so difficult to get.

‌



“You know you are one game away to be able to lift a trophy. Obviously, it’s going to be a tough game, a very difficult game, but we’ll go for it. I have played in the League Cup final before, but I have played many finals in my career, I have been lucky in that way.

“So I will just approach the game the best way that I can. We will prepare everything in the best way, because at the end the aim is to win.

“Just focus on the game, focus on us and prepare for the opponent. That’s it. I don’t think we need to approach anything differently.”

‌



Meanwhile, Arsenal defender Piero Hincapie says their Champions League win over his former club Bayer Leverkusen - including Eberechi Eze’s wonder strike - will really put them in the mood for the rest of the campaign.

View 3 Images Arsenal had the perfect prep for Sunday's Wembley showdown after making light work of Bayer Leverkusen in the Champions League (Image: Arsenal FC via Getty Images)

Hincapie said: “I'm really excited to have qualified for the quarter-finals with my club. I'm also a bit sad because we played the phase with my former club. But this is football and I'm really happy now.

‌



“We're really focused on the final on Sunday, which is a very important final. We're going to train very hard to get to the final in the best way possible and to win the title. That’s the most important thing. We're very focused on wanting to win it, but we have to work hard to win the title.

“It was a good goal. Fantastic. It was amazing. I didn’t know it was his first goal. It was an amazing goal.”

Football.title: Jeremy Jacquet injury timeline emerges as Liverpool 'panic' over injury recovery time
Football.content:
Jeremy Jacquet injury timeline emerges as Liverpool 'panic' over injury recovery time

Liverpool's £60m summer signing Jeremy Jacquet underwent shoulder surgery after fracturing his shoulder against Lens last month

View Image Jeremy Jacquet will join Liverpool this summer (Image: Neal Simpson/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images)

Jeremy Jacquet has undergone surgery following a shoulder fracture last month, and Liverpool have now been informed of the length of his recovery period. The soon-to-be Reds' summer signing sustained the injury during a match against Lens.

‌



The seriousness of the injury meant that surgery was necessary, effectively concluding his stint at the Ligue 1 club. On 12 March, Rennes confirmed in a brief statement that the surgery had been successful.

‌



"Successful intervention in Paris, speedy recovery," it stated. Whilst the Ligue 1 team hasn't provided a specific timeline for the young player's return, French publication Sport has suggested that Jacquet is likely to be sidelined for between eight and 12 weeks.

‌



The same report indicated that Liverpool were 'panicking' about the extent of Jacquet's injury, as the club had 'already been envisioning him forming a partnership' with Virgil van Dijk at the start of the next season.

However, his recovery is expected to delay his integration into the team, with the defender likely to have a light pre-season and arrive lacking match fitness, reports the Liverpool Echo.

Liverpool outbid Chelsea for the defender's signature, agreeing a £60million deal with Rennes - £55m paid upfront and an additional £5m due in add-ons.

‌



Jacquet's impending arrival at Anfield comes as Liverpool are gearing up for another reshuffle in their starting XI, this time in defence.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Last summer, the club recruited Giovanni Leoni from Parma, but his debut season was abruptly halted when he suffered a severe knee injury against Southampton in the Carabao Cup.

‌



Speaking last month about the signing of Jacquet, Arne Slot said: "Very pleased because first of all he’s a very big talent, maybe even more than a talent – but we speak about talent because of his age of course.

"Second of all, because we weren’t the only ones interested in him. So another big compliment for the people working every single day, so hard, that we were able to sign such a big talent.

"Another example of the model we are using at this club. Young, very talented players, sometimes at the start of their careers, sometimes already a few years in.

Article continues below

"But always players that are young and can improve us and help us for the short but definitely also for the long term. We've signed a lot of them recently and I’ve said many times that the mid-to-long-term future, but even the short-term future, of this club is in a very, very good place."

Football.title: Richard Hughes 'on shortlist' for Saudi job as Liverpool's private thoughts emerge
Football.content:
Richard Hughes 'on shortlist' for Saudi job as Liverpool's private thoughts emerge

Liverpool could soon be contacted by Saudi Arabian giants Al-Hilal, who have shortlisted club chief Richard Hughes for the role of sporting director this summer

View 2 Images Richard Hughes is wanted for a new job (Image: Robin Jones/Getty Images)

Saudi Arabian side Al-Hilal are interested in hiring Liverpool sporting director Richard Hughes, despite a rocky second season at the club. Hughes joined the Reds at the end of the 2023/24 campaign and has been a hugely influential figure during the Arne Slot era.

‌



As sporting director, Hughes was behind Liverpool's eye-catching summer transfer window when record fees were spent on Florian Wirtz and Alexander Isak. Liverpool splashed out around £449million in transfer fees, with Jeremie Frimpong, Hugo Ekitike, Milos Kerkez and Giovanni Leoni also arriving in a major squad overhaul after winning the Premier League title in Slot's first season.

‌



Things haven't gone to plan since, with Liverpool's title defence over before it started and the new signings taking time to settle. In January, reports first emerged linking Hughes with a move to the Saudi Pro League - and they have resurfaced once more.

‌



Hughes is under contract with Liverpool until June 2027, but reports from the Middle East claimed he had agreed to join Al-Hilal. The Telegraph have now reported that he is, in fact, on a three-man shortlist for the role of sporting director at the Saudi side.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Pep Guardiola hits back at Real Madrid claims with Jurgen Klopp confession READ MORE :

Al-Hilal are yet to contact Liverpool and the club are understood to be content that he won't be leaving. The 46-year-old is close to Michael Edwards, the chief executive of football for Liverpool's owners Fenway Sports Group, and hasn't yet been in position for two years.

‌



Were he to agree to join Al-Hilal, Hughes would not start work until the summer. Despite being unbeaten, the Riyadh-based side are currently second in the Pro League, three points behind Cristiano Ronaldo's Al-Nassr.

They are owned by the country's Public Investment Fund, managed by former Inter Milan boss Simone Inzaghi and the team contains Karim Benzema, Ruben Neves, Joao Cancelo, Kalidou Koulibaly and former Liverpool striker Darwin Nunez.

View 2 Images Richard Hughes has worked with Arne Slot to build Liverpool's current squad (Image: Andrew Powell, Liverpool FC via Getty Images)

‌



Hughes may already be familiar with Al-Hilal given they are one of the Saudi Arabian clubs who have shown an interest in Liverpool star Mohamed Salah. That interest has existed ever since 2023, when the Reds rejected a £150m bid from Al-Ittihad for the Egyptian star, and peaked again in December after Salah fell out with Slot.

Hughes was hired by Edwards from a similar role at Bournemouth, where he oversaw the hiring of Andoni Iraola. He said he was "incredibly proud" to join Liverpool, where he has been backed with huge financial power by FSG.

Liverpool are fifth in the Premier League, 21 points behind leaders Arsenal, and rather than fighting for the title, they are battling for a place in next season's Champions League. They will have to overturn a 1-0 deficit in the second leg of their last-16 tie against Galatasaray at Anfield on Wednesday night and face Manchester City in the quarter finals of the FA Cup.

Football.title: Why Senegal can fight AFCON final decision as new twist emerges from U-turn carnage
Football.content:
Why Senegal can fight AFCON final decision as new twist emerges from U-turn carnage

Morocco were named champions of the 2025 African Cup of Nations after the CAF stripped Senegal of the title following their 1-0 win on January 18

View 2 Images Senegal's AFCON win earlier this year has been stripped by the CAF (Image: Getty Images)

Senegal are reportedly likely to argue against the Confederation of African Football (CAF) ruling to overturn the result of the 2025 African Cup of Nations final and declare Morocco winners. Senegal won the final 1-0 on January 18, a match overshadowed by major controversy.

‌



A 17-minute delay occurred late in the contest after VAR awarded the host nation, Morocco, a penalty for a soft foul. Moments earlier, Senegal had a goal ruled out for another contentious call from referee Jean-Jacques Ndala.

‌



The Senegal players refused to play, with some leaving the field and returning to the changing room. After returning, Brahim Diaz's penalty was saved before Pape Gueye scored an extra-time winner to win their second AFCON title.

‌



AFCON final result REVERSED after Senegal chaos as extraordinary statement issued READ MORE :

AFCON statement in full as Morocco named champions and Senegal sanctions confirmed READ MORE :

However, two months after the final, the CAF confirmed that the result had now been overturned following a decision by an appeal board at the governing body. The statement claimed that Senegal had been "declared to have forfeited the final match", with Morocco being handed a 3-0 win as a result.

The Senegalese Football Federation have confirmed they would appeal against the ruling at the Court of Arbitration for Sport, labelling it "an unfair, unprecedented and unacceptable decision which brings discredit to African football".

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



According to journalist Melissa Reddy, the federation are likely to argue that they did not abandon the result and the game was completed by the referee's whistle. "They can say Article 82 was misapplied because the game continued under the same circumstances and reached its natural end," she wrote on X.

"The referee allowing Senegal back on the pitch and Morocco happily continuing the game will be used to state the match - and result - was valid. As the final was completed, they can argue the integrity of the game was upheld.

View 2 Images Senegal's win over Morocco in the AFCON final has been overturned (Image: Samah Zidan/Anadolu via Getty Images)

‌



"They can also claim changing the winner of a completed final is an extreme overreach by the Appeal Board. And that the punishment for a delay - not a refusal to play as they finished the game - is excessive."

Abdoulaye Seydou Sow, secretary general of the Senegal FA, told public broadcaster Radiodiffusion Télévision Sénégalaise that they plan to get a fair ruling beyond CAF's decision. "This is a travesty; this decision is based on absolutely nothing," he said.

"It has no legal foundation … The president of the federation will get in touch with the lawyers; we will engage with the appropriate authorities, and then we will go to the court of arbitration for sport, which will issue the final decision.”

‌



Alongside the drama surrounding the controversial penalty, there were also moments that left a sour taste in the mouth. During the final, Senegal substitute goalkeeper Yehvann Diouf was forced to protect Edouard Mendy's towel by Moroccan ball boys, who were spotted dragging Diouf across the pitch at one point.

Diouf even had to fend off PSV Eindhoven and Morocco winger Ismael Saibari. The forward stood between the two goalkeepers as he attempted to hand Mendy a towel.

Post-match, Senegal boss Pape Thiaw stormed out of his press conference after receiving boos by Moroccan journalists. The 44-year-old was applauded by Senegalese reporters, but jeered by locals, resulting in Thiaw refusing to speak on the final.

Football.title: Q&A: 'Extraordinary' AFCON ruling explained and the potential ramifications
Football.content:
The extraordinary ruling to strip Senegal of their AFCON title and award it to Morocco two months later is unprecedented. Here we answer the key questions and examine the potential ramifications...

Why has the decision been taken?

The Confederation of African Football (CAF) Appeal Board found Senegal broke AFCON regulations by walking off the pitch during the final, which took place on January 18, after Morocco were awarded a contentious penalty.

Many Senegal players vacated the field, led by their coach Pape Thiaw, delaying the game by around 17 minutes before captain Sadio Mane convinced them to return. Brahim Diaz had his Panenka saved before tournament hosts Morocco lost 1-0 in extra-time.

Image: Sadio Mane urged his Senegal players back on the pitch as they left in protest

Article 82 of AFCON's regulations states that if a team refuses to play or leaves the ground before the end of regulation time without the referee's authorisation, it will be considered the loser and eliminated from the competition.

Under Article 84 any team that violates Article 82 will be permanently eliminated and lose the match 3-0.

The result is an outcome of an appeal made by Morocco's football federation.

Why has it taken two months?

No explanation has been given by CAF around why the officials failed to apply the regulations at the time.

CAF have re-analysed the rulebook following Morocco's appeal against initial sanctions around the other controversial flashpoints from the final.

Subsequently, the appeal board took the unprecedented step of reversing the result, awarding a 3-0 win to Morocco.

Morocco said in a statement the appeal was never "intended to challenge the sporting performance of the teams" only "to request the application of the competition's regulations".

"This is just completely extraordinary," Sky News' sport correspondent Rob Harris said. "You can't really think of anything like it at this level."

Is this the end of the matter? Will there be an appeal?

The Senegalese football federation plan to appeal the ruling to the Court of Arbitration for Sport (CAS), the highest court in Switzerland, which typically takes around a year to deliver a verdict.

Senegal are preparing to file their appeal and after that a panel will be appointed for their case.

How much damage has been done to AFCON's reputation?

Image: Mali players celebrate after the penalty shootout win against Tunisia

The Senegalese federation branded the decision "iniquitous, unprecedented and unacceptable", saying it "threw discredit on African football".

Secretary general of Senegal's football federation called the decision a "shame for Africa" in an interview with public broadcaster Radiodiffusion Télévision Sénégalaise.

For now, technically, Morocco are considered 3-0 winners of the final which ends a 49-year wait for their second AFCON title.

As tournament hosts, much of the build-up centered around whether Morocco, considered the favourites, could finally get over the line.

Two months later, after losing the game, Morocco have been crowned champions via a statement.

Dependent on Senegal's appeal, that honour might be snatched back from them.

Irrespective of the outcome, AFCON 2025 is tarnished by the handling of this matter and so too, by extension, the winners, whose name on the trophy will come with an asterisk as far as fans are concerned.

Image: Senegal players celebrate with the trophy after winning the Africa Cup of Nations

"It's quite sad to see how things have developed and some of the reactions because for such a long time, there's been a big debate about AFCON and African football getting the respect that it deserves," African football expert Mimi Fawaz said on Sky Sports News.

"But reading the reaction from a lot of the African football fans, that's the argument that they're making, some of them, that it has put a stain on African football.

"They have criticised the decision that was made, especially coming two months after the final where Senegal were awarded the winners.

"But of course, some Moroccan fans are saying that they're actually happy with the result. They feel that it's justified."

Are Senegal expected to hand back their medals?

This much is unclear for now, with an appeal process set to commence, however judging by the Senegal response it would appear that might be a difficult task.

Senegal's men's team responded to Tuesday night's ruling by posting footage of their victory parade on X.

Some of Senegal's Premier League stars also mocked the ruling on social media.

West Ham's El Hadji Malick Diouf, Crystal Palace's Ismaila Sarr and Sunderland's Habib Diarra all posted laughing emojis on their Instagram stories, as well as pictures of them with the AFCON trophy and celebrations.

How have Morocco's players responded?

As it stands, none of the star players of Morocco's squad have celebrated the AFCON final result being reversed.

Paris Saint-Germain's Achraf Hakimi and Real Madrid's Brahim Diaz were in Champions League action when the ruling was announced.

Footage circulating on social media appears to show Brahim, whose panenka penalty was saved at 0-0, being informed of the decision. He is shown laughing and lifting his arms in celebration, seemingly in jest.

Image: Brahim Diaz failed to score from the spot

What if you bet on Morocco to win?

Bookmaker Paddy Power posted on X to say that any single or multiple bets on Morocco to win would now be paid out. Refunds are not being requested for those who backed Senegal to win.

Sky Bet have paid out on Morocco as winners in the outright market.

Betfair say they have resettled outrights bets on Morocco placed before the tournament.

Football.title: Sabalenka criticises Dubai tournament director and threatens not to return
Football.content:
World No 1 Aryna Sabalenka said she is unsure about returning to Dubai after the tournament director criticised ‌her withdrawal - labelling the demand for tougher penalties on late pull-outs a "ridiculous" idea.

Sabalenka and world No 3 Iga Swiatek were among several players ruled out ‌due to injury, illness or scheduling changes, leaving lucky losers to fill the depleted main draw in Dubai and taking ​gloss off the WTA 1000 event only below the Grand Slams in stature.

Indian Wells champion Sabalenka cited a minor hip injury when she pulled out of the tournament last month, ​before the US-Israeli war with Iran sparked chaos in the region.

At the time, ‌Dubai tournament director Salah Tahlak told The National that Sabalenka and Swiatek pulling out had been an "unfortunate surprise" for the organisers and ​sought strict action for late withdrawals with ranking points being docked.

"I think it's ridiculous," Sabalenka told reporters at the Miami Open. "I don't think he showed himself in the best way possible. ​For me it's actually so sad to see that the tournament directors and ⁠the tournaments not protecting us as players. They just ⁠care about their (sales), about their tournament and that's it.

"His comment was ridiculous. I'm not sure if I ever want to go there after his comment. For ‌me it's too much."

Top players are obliged to compete in all four Grand Slams, 10 WTA 1000 tournaments and six WTA 500 events under WTA rules, with ‌punishments for missing them ranging from ranking points penalties to fines.

Gauff: The comments were unnecessary

Image: Coco Gauff said 'we're trying our best ⁠to play the calendar'

Coco Gauff urged a more understanding view of Swiatek and Sabalenka pulling out of Dubai.

"I just feel Iga and Aryna have played that tournament so many times and it wasn't anything personal to it," Gauff said.

"It's tough. We're trying our best ⁠to play the calendar. I completely understand why she (Sabalenka) would feel like that because ​the comments were unnecessary."

The ATP and WTA have faced criticism due ​to their 11-month seasons, and both came under scrutiny towards the end of last year ​with several matches in the Asian swing unable to be completed due to injuries.

Image: Sabalenka is defending her Miami Open title

Sabalenka, who will defend her Miami Open trophy, said she had opted to be more selective this season to negotiate the demands of a relentless circuit.

"Going into this season, we decided to prioritise my ⁠health and make sure we have these little gaps in ​the schedule where I can reset, recharge, work and be better prepared for bigger tournaments," ​said Sabalenka, who also skipped Doha.

"I feel like the scheduling is going crazy and that's why you see so many players injured, always taped and not delivering ‌the best quality matches because it's ​almost impossible."

Sabalenka's remarkable tie-break career record at Grand Slams and Masters 1000 Tournament Tie-Break won Tie-Break lost US Open 10 3 Australian Open 8 3 French Open 7 2 Wimbledon 5 1 Cincinnati 5 3 Indian Wells 4 2 Madrid 4 2 Wuhan Open 3 1 Miami Open 3 0 China Open 3 2 Canadian Open 3 3 Rome 2 1 Qatar Open 2 1 Dubai 1 3

Sabalenka: Indian Wells win helps me grow

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of the 2026 Indian Wells final between Aryna Sabalenka and Elena Rybakina

Speaking to Sky Sports' Laura Robson, Sabalenka reflected on her epic victory over Elena Rybakina in California, saying: "It was a crazy match, but those are the kind of finals I want to play because in those kind of moments you grow and you get better as a player and as a person as well.

"It was an important win for me. I lost the Indian Wells final two times so I didn't want it to happen again. I wanted to prove to myself I can come back stronger."

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Sabalenka was joined by her adorable puppy on court after sealing the Indian Wells title

Four-time Grand Slam champion Sabalenka now has her sights set on completing the 'Sunshine Double'.

Only four women have achieved the unique double - Steffi Graf (1994 and 1996), Kim Clijsters (2005), Victoria Azarenka (2016) and, most recently, Iga Swiatek (2022).

"It would be huge but who knows what the future holds, " said Sabalenka. "I would love to achieve that but I just want to take it one step at a time."

Football.title: 'I wanted revenge' - Boulter continues revival with hard-fought win at Miami Open
Football.content:
Katie Boulter continued her revival with a hard-fought victory over Jessica Bouzas Maneiro in the opening round of the Miami Open to gain sweet revenge for her US Open defeat to the Spaniard in 2024.

Boulter, who received direct entry into the main draw after enjoying a consistent couple of months on the WTA Tour, fought back from 6-2 down in the first-set tie break to overcome her Spanish opponent 7-6 (11-9) 6-4.

The British No 3 made a strong start in the main draw, progressing in one hour and 48 minutes to set up a meeting with 17th seed Clara Tauson in the second round.

The 29-year-old picked up her fourth tour-level title last month at the Ostrava Open and she backed up that run by reaching the quarter-finals at the Merida Open.

Despite losing in qualifying at Indian Wells, Boulter put on a strong showing in Florida saving six set points in a prolonged opening set tie-break, eventually closing it out after more than an hour of play.

Boulter maintained her level and despite being broken early in the second set, she responded by breaking back and serving out the match.

Tale of the Tape

Boulter: I wanted revenge

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Katie Boutler was pleased with her opening round victory over Jessica Bouzas Manerio

"It was unfortunate I got broken from love-40. I thought I played a really good game and I kind of gave a couple of points away on double faults. It was a small mistake but I didn't let that control so I focused on myself and managed to go for it in the last game and trust my shots," Boulter told Sky Sports' Laura Robson.

"It's really difficult out here. It's cold, it's windy but you still want to commit and she is a player who goes after her shots a lot and if you don't go after it she's going to control you and you're going to get into a bit of trouble. Whenever I got broken she was going for her shots and I think she played some great tennis.

"It is tricky with the serve, you have kind of got to keep your first serve percentage up and try and get a bit of shape on it which I felt I did well at times and stuck to the right shots I was trying to hit throughout the rest."

Boulter, who was beaten by Bouzas Maneiro at the US Open in 2024, admitted their previous meeting has been playing on her mind.

"That kind of stung me and I wanted revenge," Boulter said. "It was the first thing I thought off when I saw the draw. I actually let that one get away from me but I know how good a player she is and I felt like the level and quality of our tennis tonight was good but really pleased with the way I played today."

Brits in action on Wednesday

Image: Francesca Jones takes on legendary Venus Williams at the Miami Open on Wednesday

Three-time winner Venus Williams returns for her 23rd Miami Open against Britain's Fran Jones, while Arthur Fery, who has qualified for his first ever Masters 1000, takes on former French Open finalist Stefanos Tsitsipas.

Football.title: McIlroy's Boston lose out to Jupiter Links as Fleetwood sends LA to TGL final
Football.content:
Jupiter Links GC defeated Rory McIlroy's Boston Common to reach the TGL final against Los Angeles' Tommy Fleetwood and Justin Rose.

Tiger Woods continued to sit out of the 2026 season as he recovers from injury, while McIlroy returned to action after his unsuccessful title defence at The Players.

Jupiter Links GC, who finished in last in the inaugural season of TGL, upset McIlroy's Boston Common 9-5 at the SoFi Center, as Los Angeles Golf Club dethroned defending TGL champion Atlanta Drive 6-4 led by Fleetwood's strategic use of the Hammer on the 14th hole and several clutch putts from Rose.

"Now the 'Bad News Bears' can play," Woods said. "So we're back and we're now advancing into the finals and it's been incredible.

"As I said, the bonding that has, that occurs - no matter what team you're on whether you're playing for Ryder Cup or playing Presidents Cup, World Cups, whatever it is, the bonding that happens in this, in teams, it's so different and unique."

Akshay Bhatia, signed with Jupiter Links as an alternate in January, has shot par or better in 20 straight PGA Tour rounds since the start of February. He starred alongside Max Homa, Tom Kim, and Kevin Kisner.

"I think the bond is the biggest thing," Bhatia said. "I've definitely gotten a lot closer with Max, especially at tournaments. Now I feel like every practice round we're texting each other, and TK same thing.

"Obviously to have the greatest player of all time in your corner is really, really cool. I'm glad that I was an alternate for this team because this is definitely the kind of the best, you know, gathering of guys that I could have imagined."

Homa, who was matched up against McIlroy in singles, delivered the clean sweep of their two holes together.

His par-saver on No. 13, where he hit the Steph Curry 'Night night' celebration, proved to be the match-winning point.

"It's kind of win or go home. Unfortunately, we're on the 'go home' end of that tonight," said Adam Scott, who played alongside McIlroy and Keegan Bradley.

Los Angeles overcame an early deficit with Rose nailing a 24-foot birdie to get Los Angeles on the board before the 45-year-old handed his team the lead for the first time in the match with a birdie at No 14.

Fleetwood's strategic use of the Hammer on the 14th hole was a pivotal moment in the match.

Los Angeles had been leading 5-4 and threw the hammer with Billy Horschel needing to make a par to tie.

Patrick Cantlay tried to convince Billy Horschel to accept the hammer, so the team had a clean chance to win on No 15, but Horschel declined, and the team fell behind 6-4 and with it, the match.

Rose called it "genius" after saying he had no clue what was happening.

"I was unaware of it," he said. "Next thing I knew the thing [Hammer] was on the ground. So it was like, 'What the hell's going on here?'"

"It just felt like the right thing to do," Fleetwood said. "It obviously gave them a pretty tough decision to make. Maybe I got lucky with thinking the right way at the right time."
